seo.title: Building RAG Pipeline with Azure AI Agent Service + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build rag pipeline with Azure AI Agent Service + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.
keywords: Azure AI Agent Service rag pipeline

content.intro: Azure AI Agent Service enables enterprise-grade RAG pipelines by combining Azure OpenAI models with built-in grounding, vector search, and enterprise security controls. Integrating Vex as middleware ensures reliable agent outputs by validating tool results, detecting hallucinations, and enforcing policy compliance across the retrieval-augmentation-synthesis flow. Guardrails are critical for high-risk use cases where source faithfulness and citation accuracy directly impact business decisions.

architectureOverview.heading: RAG Pipeline Architecture with Azure AI Agent Service
architectureOverview.description:
The Azure AI Agent Service RAG pipeline follows a standard retrieval-augmented generation pattern: user queries trigger vector search across indexed enterprise documents, retrieve relevant chunks with metadata, assemble context windows, and pass to Azure OpenAI for synthesis with source attribution. Agents leverage built-in tools for Bing Search grounding and file retrieval, orchestrated through agent threads with conversation memory. Vex integrates as middleware between tool execution and LLM synthesis, validating outputs against enterprise policies before context assembly.

Key components include Azure AI Search for vector indexing, Azure OpenAI for embedding and generation, and Agent Service orchestration layer managing tool calls, thread state, and response formatting. The pipeline handles token limits through dynamic context truncation but lacks built-in validation for content freshness, policy compliance, or hallucination detection—precisely where Vex adds value.

Enterprise security flows through Azure RBAC and content safety filters, but tool outputs (Bing results, file metadata) require additional validation to prevent policy violations or stale information propagation.
architectureOverview.diagram:
┌─────────────────┐    ┌──────────────────┐    ┌──────────────────┐
│   User Query    │───▶│ Vector Search    │───▶│ Context Assembly │
└─────────────────┘    │ (Azure AI Search) │    │   + Vex Guard    │
                       └──────────────────┘    │   Validation     │
                                               └──────────┬───────┘
                                                          │
                                               ┌──────────▼───────┐
                                               │ Azure OpenAI     │
                                               │   Synthesis      │
                                               │  + Citations     │
                                               └──────────────────┘

Tools: Bing Search ────── File Retrieval ────── Vex Middleware ────── Agent Thread

risks.name: Tool execution results not validated against enterprise policies
risks.description: Azure Agent tools (Bing Search, file retrieval) return unfiltered results that may violate DLP policies or contain PII
risks.mitigation: Vex DLP scanner intercepts tool outputs before context assembly

risks.name: Grounding data from Bing Search containing outdated information
risks.description: Real-time Bing results may include stale or contradictory enterprise knowledge
risks.mitigation: Vex freshness validator cross-checks against internal knowledge base

risks.name: File search returning Azure-specific metadata instead of content
risks.description: Vector search prioritizes metadata fields over actual document content
risks.mitigation: Vex content validator ensures retrieved chunks contain substantive text

risks.name: Agent thread token limits exceeded without graceful degradation
risks.description: Long conversations exceed context windows, causing truncation or errors
risks.mitigation: Vex token monitor triggers summarization before limit breach

implementation.title: Initialize Azure AI Agent Service Client
implementation.description: Create agent client with RAG extensions and Vex middleware integration
implementation.code:
from azure.ai.projects import AIProjectsClient
from vex_sdk import VexClient

# Azure AI Agent Service client
client = AIProjectsClient(
    credential=DefaultAzureCredential(),
    subscription_id="your-sub-id",
    resource_group="rg-agent-service",
    project_name="rag-pipeline-project"
)

# Vex middleware client
vex = VexClient(api_key="vex-api-key")

agent = client.agents.get(name="rag-agent")
agent.add_extension(vex.middleware())

print(f"RAG Agent with Vex guardrails ready: {agent.name}")

implementation.title: Vector Search with Content Validation
implementation.description: Execute semantic search and validate retrieved chunks contain actual content
implementation.code:
search_results = agent.tools.file_search.run(
    query=user_query,
    top_k=5,
    vector_fields=["content"]
)

# Vex validates content vs metadata
validated_chunks = vex.validate(
    inputs=search_results.chunks,
    checks=[
        "content_substance",
        "dlp_policy",
        "source_freshness"
    ]
)

context = "\n".join([chunk.content for chunk in validated_chunks])

implementation.description: Create conversation thread with automatic Vex validation on each turn

implementation.title: Context Assembly with Token Monitoring
implementation.description: Dynamically truncate context to prevent token limit violations

vexIntegration.title: Policy Validation Middleware
vexIntegration.description: Intercept all tool outputs for enterprise policy compliance
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk.policies import PolicyValidator

@vex.middleware()
def policy_guard(tool_output):
    validator = PolicyValidator([
        "no_pii",
        "no_toxicity",
        "domain_restriction:enterprise.com"
    ])
    
    results = validator.scan(tool_output)
    
    if not results.passed:
        raise PolicyViolation(
            f"Blocked: {results.violations}",
            safe_alternative=results.redacted_content
        )
    
    return tool_output

# Register with agent
agent.extensions.add(policy_guard)

vexIntegration.title: Source Faithfulness Check
vexIntegration.description: Verify LLM response stays faithful to retrieved sources
vexIntegration.code:
faithfulness_results = vex.evaluate(
    response=llm_output,
    sources=context_chunks,
    metric="source_faithfulness"
)

if faithfulness_results.score < 0.85:
    raise GuardrailViolation(
        f"Low faithfulness: {faithfulness_results.score:.2f}",
        evidence=faithfulness_results.explanation
    )

# Add attribution
llm_output.metadata.citations = faithfulness_results.citations

vexIntegration.title: TypeScript Hallucination + Citation Guard
